findings.description: The metadata_json column on the contents table is declared as sa.JSON() (SQLAlchemy JSON type), which maps to PostgreSQL 'json' type, NOT 'jsonb'. GIN indexes (jsonb_path_ops) and the @> containment operator require jsonb. The _find_by_external_id method and GIN index creation will fail against a json column.
findings.resolution: Add an ALTER TABLE contents ALTER COLUMN metadata_json TYPE jsonb USING metadata_json::jsonb step to the migration before CREATE INDEX ... USING GIN. Update the SQLAlchemy model to use JSONB instead of JSON for metadata_json. Coordinate with Scholar/arXiv proposals so only the first migration performs the alter.

findings.description: The unique constraint uq_content_reference UNIQUE (source_content_id, external_id, external_id_type) does not account for NULL values. In SQL, NULL != NULL, so multiple URL-only references (external_id=NULL) from the same source won't be caught. The partial unique index on external_url handles some cases but nothing prevents references with both external_id=NULL and external_url=NULL.
findings.resolution: Add CHECK constraint: CONSTRAINT chk_has_identifier CHECK (external_id IS NOT NULL OR external_url IS NOT NULL). This makes the two unique constraints collectively exhaustive.

findings.description: CLAUDE.md gotcha: autoflush=False + dedup loop. The store_references function adds references in a loop relying on unique constraints. With autoflush=False, newly added rows within the same batch won't be visible to dedup checks, causing collisions at commit.
findings.resolution: Use INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING via SQLAlchemy's insert().on_conflict_do_nothing() to handle duplicates atomically. Mandate this conflict-handling strategy in the spec.

findings.description: Three new PostgreSQL enum types are introduced (referencetype, externalidtype, resolutionstatus). CLAUDE.md warns: PG enum + Python StrEnum mismatch requires ALTER TYPE migrations for each new value. Future additions to these enums are very likely.
findings.resolution: Use VARCHAR columns with application-level StrEnum validation instead of PG enum types. This avoids the migration burden. The design SQL already uses VARCHAR(20) — align the spec with the design.

findings.description: The design SQL schema uses VARCHAR(20) for reference_type, external_id_type, and resolution_status, but spec REQ-REF-010 says 'Add referencetype, externalidtype, resolutionstatus PostgreSQL enum types'. Design and spec contradict each other.
findings.resolution: Align spec REQ-REF-010 with the design's VARCHAR approach. Remove the requirement to create PostgreSQL enum types.

findings.description: The _find_chunk_for_offset function matches regex character offsets from markdown_content against DocumentChunk.start_char/end_char. If the chunker modifies whitespace, strips headers, or transforms text before recording offsets, positions won't align.
findings.resolution: Verify that DocumentChunk.start_char/end_char are character offsets into the original markdown_content field. Add spec assertion: 'start_char and end_char SHALL be character offsets into the original markdown_content.'

findings.description: resolve_incoming runs three separate queries per content ingestion (arxiv_id, doi, source_url). The partial index ix_content_refs_unresolved may not cover these queries efficiently.
findings.resolution: The existing ix_content_refs_external_id index should cover structured ID lookups. Add resolution_status filter to queries. Monitor query plans; consider composite partial index if needed.

findings.description: Auto-ingest depth check uses metadata_json.get('ingestion_mode') == 'auto_ingest' as sole guard. The max_depth parameter is declared but never used. If metadata is NULL or not set correctly (bug), recursive ingestion proceeds.
findings.resolution: Track actual depth via auto_ingest_depth: int in metadata_json (0 for user-ingested, 1 for auto-ingest, etc.) and compare against max_depth. More robust than boolean check and supports future depth > 1.

findings.description: REQ-REF-007 specifies bidirectional supplements references but design only creates one direction (Scholar → arXiv). For true bidirectionality, two rows are needed or the query layer must treat supplements as symmetric.
findings.resolution: Either create two content_reference rows (one each direction), or document that supplements is symmetric and queries must search both source_content_id and target_content_id.

findings.description: The partial unique constraint uq_content_reference_url uses WHERE clause syntax (UNIQUE ... WHERE external_id IS NULL). Standard SQL UNIQUE constraints do not support WHERE. This must be a partial unique index, not a table constraint.
findings.resolution: Change to CREATE UNIQUE INDEX uq_content_reference_url ON content_references (source_content_id, external_url) WHERE external_id IS NULL. Use op.create_index(unique=True, postgresql_where=...) in Alembic.

findings.description: The resolve_references handler must be registered in register_all_handlers() in src/queue/worker.py. The design does not show the import wiring. Existing handlers are registered inline in _register_content_handlers().
findings.resolution: Add a _register_reference_handlers() function call in register_all_handlers() that imports the handler module, following existing pattern.

findings.description: ReferenceGraphSync creates its own Neo4j driver instance, but the codebase uses Graphiti which manages its own connection. This introduces a second connection pool outside Graphiti's management.
findings.resolution: Reuse the existing GraphitiClient.driver instance for citation edge operations instead of creating a separate driver. GraphitiClient already exposes self.driver for raw Cypher queries.

findings.description: Fail-safe spec (REQ-REF-012) does not specify whether partial extraction results should be persisted if extraction fails mid-way through processing.
findings.resolution: Clarify: partial results are persisted (store what succeeded), errors logged at WARNING, no automatic retry.

findings.description: arXiv ID regex patterns only match new-format IDs (YYMM.NNNNN, post-2007). Old-format IDs (hep-ph/0601001) are not captured.
findings.resolution: Add a note: 'Reference extraction supports new-format arXiv IDs (YYMM.NNNNN, April 2007+) only.'

findings.description: DOI regex pattern r'DOI:\s*(10\.\d{4,9}/[^\s\)]+)' uses greedy match that captures trailing punctuation. normalize_id strips some but may miss edge cases.
findings.resolution: Tighten regex to exclude common terminators from capture group, or ensure normalize_id handles all edge cases.

findings.description: Tasks dependency graph states 'Max parallel width: 3 (sections 1, 2, 9 in first wave)' but Section 9 (MCP Tools) depends on 7 and 8. First wave should be 1, 2, and 10 (Settings).
findings.resolution: Fix the parallel width statement to reference correct sections.

findings.description: REQ-REF-008 API endpoints do not mention pagination parameters. Other API routes use page/page_size with standard pagination.
findings.resolution: Add pagination parameters and metadata to API endpoint spec.

findings.description: MCP tool resolve_references accepts auto_ingest parameter that could override system-level reference_auto_ingest_enabled setting. Precedence not specified.
findings.resolution: Clarify: MCP tool auto_ingest parameter only effective when setting is True. If setting is False, parameter is ignored.

findings.description: If chunks are re-indexed (deleted and recreated) without deleting content, source_chunk_id becomes NULL via ON DELETE SET NULL. The spec mentions 'retroactive anchoring when chunks become available' (REQ-REF-001) but provides no mechanism for this update.
findings.resolution: Add a task or post-chunking hook that re-anchors references when chunks are re-indexed for a content item: call _find_chunk_for_offset on all references with source_chunk_id IS NULL for the content_id.
